exceptions.reason: Renders role="alertdialog", not "dialog" -- arguably the more correct choice for a destructive-action confirmation (APG treats alertdialog as dialog's specialisation for exactly this case), but it is a real deviation from this pattern's literal requirement, which the shared dialog-modal contract does not carve out.
No aria-labelledby or aria-label -- `title` and `eyebrow` render as plain styled divs with no id referenced anywhere.
Only met when `requireText` is set: the confirmation input carries autoFocus and is the first focusable element in DOM order. Without `requireText` -- the common case -- nothing moves focus at all.
No stored reference to the previously-focused element, and no code that restores it on close.
No keydown handler constrains Tab; focus can walk straight out of the dialog into the page behind it.

Not implemented -- no keydown listener anywhere. This is compounded by the component's own documented choice not to close on click-outside either ("Does NOT close on click-outside (avoids losses)"), so a keyboard-only user currently has no way to cancel except tabbing to the Cancel button and activating it.
